commit.message:
test: add security invariant tests for no-key-leakage (#168)

* test: add security invariant tests for no-key-leakage

Add comprehensive tests verifying the no-key-leakage design invariant:

Core tests (crates/uselesskey-core/tests/security_invariants.rs):
- Seed Debug always outputs 'Seed(**redacted**)'
- Factory Debug never contains seed hex bytes or cached values
- Mode::Deterministic Debug shows redacted seed
- Error messages don't echo raw input
- Panic messages from type mismatches don't leak seeds

Integration tests (tests/security_invariants.rs):
- RSA, ECDSA, Ed25519: Debug doesn't contain base64 private/public key bytes
- HMAC: Debug doesn't contain base64 secret bytes or hex prefix
- Token: Debug doesn't contain token value for all spec variants
- All key types: Debug never contains PEM headers
- Generated key bytes not found in committed source files
- Debug output length is bounded (no accidental key dumps)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve clippy warnings in security invariant tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Make skill action schemas automation-ready with required data fields (#3548)

* Make add-comment risk dynamic based on content and confidence

Previously, all add-comment actions were hardcoded as 'high' risk regardless
of content. This change makes the risk level dynamic:

- low: factual content (repro findings, version matrix) with confidence >= 0.85
- medium: workaround/suggestion content with confidence >= 0.85
- high: close/reject content, or confidence < 0.70

Updated in both triage and repro schema cheatsheets and schema definitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make skill action schemas automation-ready with required data fields

- Add stateReason, category, milestone fields to both schemas
- Remove update-project (not automatable)
- Add Action Data Requirements section to repro cheatsheet
- Document required fields for every action type in both cheatsheets
- Prevents hollow actions (correct type but missing data)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent install before init with user-friendly error and hints (#228)

* Initial plan

* Prevent install before init with user-friendly error and hints

When `wasm install` is called without a local `wasm.toml` manifest,
abort early with a clear error message and two help hints:
- Suggest running `wasm init` to create the manifest
- Suggest running `wasm registry fetch <component>` as an alternative

Adds spec requirement r[install.no-manifest] and updates the existing
test snapshot to match the new error format.

commit.message:
security: move DB backups to private R2 bucket + block CDN backup paths

- Backups now target 'catananti-backups' (private, S3 API auth only)
- NOT 'catananti-assets' (public via cdn.catananti.dev)
- Added nginx deny rules for /cdn/backups/ and /cdn/private/
- All existing backups migrated and deleted from public bucket

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(inference): add DenseLinear/FFN forward_into + BlockWorkspace (#3338)

## What Changed

Add zero-allocation forward paths for the dense inference hot loop:

- **\DenseLinear::forward_into(x, out)\**: write matmul result into
pre-allocated buffer instead of returning a new Vec each call
- **\DenseFFN::forward_into(x, out, gate_buf, up_buf)\**: reuse
intermediate buffers for gate/up/down projections (eliminates 3 Vec
allocs per layer)
- **\BlockWorkspace\**: holds normed, ffn_out, ffn_gate, ffn_up scratch
buffers
- **\DenseTransformerBlock::forward_into_ws(x, out, ws)\**: uses
BlockWorkspace to eliminate all FFN allocations per layer
- **\DenseModel::forward()\**: now creates one BlockWorkspace reused
across all transformer blocks

### Impact

Eliminates **5 Vec allocations per transformer block per forward pass**
(normed, ffn_out, gate, up, down result). For a 32-layer model: 160
fewer allocations per inference.

Existing \ orward()\ and \ orward_into()\ APIs preserved for backward
compatibility.

## What I Ran Locally

\\\
cargo check -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu #
OK
cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- forward_into # 4/4 pass
cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- model_forward_uses_workspace # 1/1 pass
cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- dense_forward # 24/24 pass (all existing + new)
cargo clippy -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu --
-D warnings # clean
\\\

## CI Jobs Relied On

CI Core required gates (Build & Test, BDD Grid Check, Clippy,
Documentation, CI Core Success)

## Parity Evidence

- \ est_forward_into_ws_matches_forward_into\: verifies workspace path
produces identical output to non-workspace path
- \ est_linear_forward_into_matches_forward\: forward_into matches
forward (single sample)
- \ est_linear_forward_into_batch\: forward_into matches forward (batch
+ bias)
- \ est_ffn_forward_into_matches_forward\: FFN forward_into matches FFN
forward
- \ est_model_forward_uses_workspace\: model-level determinism with
workspace

## Docs Touched

None (internal API addition).

## Risks

Low  additive change. Existing APIs unchanged. All 24 tests pass.

## Recommended Disposition

**A) MERGE NOW** foundation for further alloc elimination (attention
projections next).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reorder setup guide: Fabric before deploy

New step order:
  1. Clone repo
  2. Configure basic env vars (region, subscription, email)
  3. Set up Fabric workspace + lakehouse (get IDs)
  4. Deploy infrastructure (azd up — with Fabric IDs already set)
  5. Create Power Automate flow

This eliminates the chicken-and-egg problem where azd up deployed
the Function App without Fabric IDs, requiring a redeploy after
Step 4. Now Fabric IDs are set before the first deploy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: current_tx_power type uint8→int8 for signed dBm

PA-equipped devices (CC2530+RFX2401C) store signed dBm values
(-22 to +4) in network_metrics.current_tx_power. The uint8 type
caused -22 to be misreported as 234 via ZCL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): set NO_COLOR=1 in test env to prevent ANSI in assertions

GitHub Actions sets FORCE_COLOR=1 which causes Rich to inject ANSI codes into
typer CliRunner output. This breaks string assertions like '--limit' since Rich
renders '--limit' as '-\x1b[0m\x1b[1;36m-limit\x1b[0m'.

Setting NO_COLOR=1 disables color output in tests, making them deterministic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Redis LLM cache, CRM domain, expanded connectors (Phase 3 & 4)

Phase 3 — Redis LLM result cache:
- Add core/cache.py: transparent Redis-backed cache for LLM responses
  * dataset_fingerprint() — SHA-256 of first 1k rows (8-char hex key suffix)
  * make_cache_key(goal, df, model) — deterministic key: llm:<goal>:<model>:<fp>
  * cache_get / cache_set — lazy Redis client, graceful fallback on errors
  * cached_llm_call() — wraps any LLM callable; hits skip OpenAI entirely
  * Disabled automatically when REDIS_URL env var is absent (zero config)
  * TTL configurable via REDIS_CACHE_TTL_S env var (default 3600 s)
- Wire cached_llm_call into _call_llm in core/analysis.py; all 5 analysis
  methods now pass goal + df so repeat runs on unchanged data are instant
- Export cache functions from core/__init__.py
- tests/test_redis_cache.py: 18 tests (fingerprint, key generation,
  get/set with mocked Redis, cache hit/miss, TTL, none-not-cached,
  disabled-without-Redis) — all passing, no real Redis required

Phase 4 — CRM domain:
- Add prompts/crm_goals.yaml: 3 CRM goals with full prompt chains
    improve_lead_conversion: trend + anomaly + ranking + prediction
    reduce_churn:            trend + anomaly + ranking + prediction
    increase_upsell:         trend + ranking + prediction
- Register all 3 CRM goals in AnalysisPipeline.goal_chains
- Add _identify_metrics() entries for all 3 CRM goals (9 new KPIs)
- Expand _ingest_crm() stub with inline HubSpot and Salesforce REST API

commit.message:
Add HTTP 1 and 2 reverse proxy server with user interface (#1)

* Initial plan

* Add Bounce HTTP/1+2 reverse proxy with iced GUI

- Cargo.toml: tokio, hyper 1.x, hyper-util, http-body-util, iced 0.14, futures
- src/types.rs: ProxyEntry, ProxyConfig, ProxyEvent shared types
- src/proxy.rs: HTTP/1.1 + h2c reverse proxy using hyper-util AutoBuilder
  - Single TcpListener; AutoBuilder detects HTTP version per connection
  - Forwards to upstream, rewrites Host header, buffers bodies (1 MB cap)
  - bytes_to_string helper to avoid duplication in body capture
  - Graceful stop via oneshot; events via mpsc
- src/app.rs: iced 0.14 application
  - Config row: upstream URL, bind addr, port, Start/Stop, status bar
  - Invalid port input surfaces an error message instead of silent fallback
  - Scrollable request list with selection
  - Detail pane: request/response headers and bodies
  - Subscription::run_with bridges tokio mpsc to iced, keyed per server run
- src/main.rs: entry point
- .gitignore: excludes /target

Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix HTTPS upstream, record all requests in UI, add debug logging

- Add hyper-tls + native-tls deps so https:// upstreams work
- Use HttpsConnector instead of plain HttpConnector on the client
- All error paths (body read, request build, upstream connect, response
  body) now emit a ProxyEvent::Entry with status=502 before returning,
  so every intercepted request appears in the UI list regardless of
  success or failure
- response_status in the body-error entry is now 502 (what the client
  actually received) rather than the partial upstream status
- Path normalisation: prepend '/' when path doesn't already start with
  one, keeping the constructed upstream URI well-formed
- Add eprintln! logging throughout (bind, accept, per-request, errors)
  prefixed with [proxy] #<id> for easy terminal debugging

Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix wrong detail data (vec-index selection) and add per-item remove button

- selected_index now stores the vec index instead of entry.id, so
  entries from different server runs with the same id no longer collide
  when looking up detail data
- Detail pane uses entries.get(selected_index) — always the exact item
  clicked, regardless of duplicate ids across restarts
- Add RemoveEntry(usize) message: removes entry at given vec index,
  adjusts selected_index (cleared if removed, decremented if later)
- Each list row now shows a × button that sends RemoveEntry(idx)
- Add eprintln! for OOB SelectEntry / RemoveEntry (defensive logging)

Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add max-entries and max-body settings; merge close button flush onto item bar

- Add max_entries (String) field + MaxEntriesChanged message to App.
  When a new entry arrives, drain excess oldest entries in one O(n)
  call and adjust selected_index accordingly. Default: 200.
- Add max_body_bytes (String) field + MaxBodyChanged message to App.
  Value is parsed and stored in ProxyConfig.max_body_bytes when the
  server starts (default: 1 MB). In proxy.rs the hardcoded MAX_BODY
  constant is removed; bytes_to_string() now takes max_body: usize.
- Add a second settings row in the UI (Max entries / Max body inputs).
- Set row spacing to 0 between label-button and × button so they appear
  as a single seamless bar with no gap.

Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scrollbar overlap and add copy-to-clipboard button on detail pane

- Use Scrollbar::spacing(2) on both scrollables so the track no
  longer overlaps the content/items — the viewport is shrunk by the
  spacing value, pushing content clear of the scrollbar.
- Add Message::CopyToClipboard: the detail pane now shows a fixed
  header row (entry summary + "Copy" button) above the scrollable
  body. Pressing Copy calls format_entry_for_clipboard() and returns
  iced::clipboard::write(text) to write the full request + response
  detail to the system clipboard.

Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: DWVoid <15832035+DWVoid@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: configure trusted publisher via release environment (#92)

Scopes the npm publish job to the `release` GitHub environment, enabling
trusted publishing via OIDC and allowing environment-level protection
rules and secrets.

## Changes

- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`**: Added `environment: release` to
the `publish` job

```yaml
publish:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  environment: release
```

This gates `id-token: write` to the `release` environment, which is
required for OIDC-based provenance and enables deployment protection
rules (required reviewers, branch restrictions, etc.).

commit.message:
feat(nvim): restructure config into modular lua files (#2)

Splits the monolithic `init.lua` into a clean modular structure.

## Structure

```
nvim/
├── init.lua                    # entry point — require only
└── lua/
    ├── config/
    │   ├── options.lua         # vim.opt settings
    │   ├── keymaps.lua         # key mappings (<Space> leader)
    │   └── lazy.lua            # lazy.nvim bootstrap
    └── plugins/
        └── colorscheme.lua     # gruvbox dark
```

## Changes

- `init.lua`: reduced to 3 require calls
- `options.lua`: line numbers, indent
(expandtab/smartindent/shiftwidth=2), search, scrolloff, clipboard,
persistent undo, etc.
- `keymaps.lua`: window navigation, visual indent, line move, clear
search highlight
- `lazy.lua`: lazy.nvim bootstrap extracted from init.lua, loads
`lua/plugins/` directory
- `plugins/colorscheme.lua`: gruvbox plugin spec extracted from init.lua

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Mar 5 shadow trades — all 5 signals blocked by triangulation gate

Scanned 9 NBA + 8 NHL games. 5 raw Elo signals found:
- DET YES @ SA (Elo 73.4% vs Poly 40.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 41.8% agrees with Poly
- GS YES @ HOU (Elo 32.6% vs Poly 23.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 24.7% agrees with Poly
- NO YES @ SAC (Elo 75.3% vs Poly 65.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 63.4% agrees with Poly
- CHI YES @ PHX (Elo 25.6% vs Poly 17.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 19.2% agrees with Poly
- NYI YES @ LA (Elo 53.5% vs Poly 45.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 43.1% agrees with Poly

Triangulation gate working: in all 5 cases, sportsbooks confirm
Polymarket pricing and Elo is the outlier. 0 paper trades placed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rebuild reactjs/ section: 33 topics with consistent 8-section format (#4)

* Initial plan

* Add ReactJS topics 01-08 with comprehensive interview-ready content

- 01-components.md: Functional vs Class, PureComponent, React.memo, HOCs, composition
- 02-jsx.md: JSX syntax, createElement, Fragments, conditional rendering patterns
- 03-props.md: Passing data, destructuring, defaultProps, PropTypes, children, compound components
- 04-state.md: useState, functional updates, immutability, batching, derived state
- 05-lifecycle-hooks.md: Class lifecycle methods, useEffect equivalents, comparison table
- 06-hooks-overview.md: All 12 built-in hooks with examples and when-to-use reference
- 07-rules-of-hooks.md: Call order internals, violations, ESLint enforcement
- 08-useEffect-useLayoutEffect.md: Timing differences, cleanup, race conditions, SSR safety

Each file follows the 8-section format with 260-420 lines of content including
basic/intermediate/advanced examples, pitfalls, and interview Q&A tables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 09-16: useEffect problems, useMemo/useCallback, useReducer, useRef, custom hooks, Context API, Redux/RTK, Flux

- 09: useEffect Problems & Solutions (infinite loops, missing deps, stale closures, race conditions, cleanup)
- 10: useMemo & useCallback (memoization, performance optimization, when to use)
- 11: useReducer (reducer pattern, complex state, action types, useContext integration)
- 12: useRef & useImperativeHandle (DOM refs, mutable values, forwardRef, custom ref APIs)
- 13: Custom Hooks (useFetch, useLocalStorage, useDebounce, useWindowSize, composition)
- 14: Context API (createContext, Provider, useContext, performance, multiple contexts)
- 15: Redux & Redux Toolkit (createSlice, configureStore, createAsyncThunk, RTK Query)
- 16: Flux Pattern & Architecture (unidirectional flow, Dispatcher, Stores, Flux vs Redux)

Each file: 8 required sections, 350-500+ lines, complete code examples, navigation links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 17-24: state management through controlled/uncontrolled components

- 17: State Management Patterns (local/global state, Context, Redux, Zustand, server state)
- 18: React.memo & Performance (memoization, useCallback, useMemo, profiling)
- 19: Code Splitting & Lazy Loading (React.lazy, Suspense, route-based splitting, prefetching)
- 20: Reconciliation & Virtual DOM (diffing algorithm, keys, React Fiber, commit phases)
- 21: React DOM Rendering (createRoot, render/commit phases, batching, Concurrent Mode)
- 22: Higher-Order Components (auth, logging, theming, HOC vs hooks comparison)
- 23: Render Props (children as function, data providers, Toggle, vs hooks)
- 24: Controlled vs Uncontrolled Components (React state vs DOM state, refs, form strategies)

Each file follows the 8-section format with 193-345 lines of comprehensive content
and complete, runnable JavaScript/JSX code examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 25-33 and update README

- 25-forwarding-refs.md: React.forwardRef, useImperativeHandle, HOC pattern
- 26-error-boundaries.md: getDerivedStateFromError, componentDidCatch, react-error-boundary
- 27-react-router.md: v6 API, nested routes, protected routes, hooks
- 28-forms.md: controlled inputs, custom useForm hook, Formik, React Hook Form
- 29-dom-manipulation.md: querySelector, useRef, D3 integration, IntersectionObserver
- 30-appendChild-classList.md: DOM insertion methods, classList API, DocumentFragment
- 31-portals.md: createPortal, modals, tooltips, event bubbling behaviour
- 32-testing.md: Jest, RTL, userEvent, async testing, renderHook
- 33-data-fetching.md: fetch/axios, React Query, SWR, AbortController, race conditions
- README.md: updated with full 33-topic table of contents and study guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rebuild reactjs/ with 33 comprehensive topics in 8-section format + update main README

commit.message:
Fix test_no_optuna reinstalling optuna at wrong version (#1522)

test_no_optuna() uninstalls optuna to test graceful handling, then
reinstalls it. It was hardcoded to reinstall optuna==2.8.0 instead of
the version range used elsewhere (>=2.8.0,<=3.6.1), leaving the test
environment with a downgraded optuna for the rest of the CI run. This
caused the saved dependency snapshot to incorrectly report optuna==2.8.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(apple-silicon): deflake NEON RMSNorm proptests with bounded input range (#3337)

## Problem
NEON RMSNorm property tests fail on macOS ARM64 CI (`Build & Test (macOS
ARM64)`) with 4/16 failures:
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_unit_rms`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_preserves_sign`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_gamma_scaling`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_matches_scalar`

- Rejects 78% of generated cases (780/1000 = wasted test budget)
- Remaining cases near 1e18 still overflow f32 when squared across large
vectors

## Fix
Replace `prop::num::f32::NORMAL` with bounded `-100.0f32..100.0f32` in
all 4 RMSNorm tests. This matches the approach already used by the
LayerNorm property tests (`-10.0..10.0`) and is realistic for neural
network activation values.

## Testing
- 5 consecutive local runs: **22/22 pass, 0 failures** ✅
- No test budget wasted on unrealistic input ranges
- Zero prop_assume rejections

Labels: apple-silicon, test-fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
